Category-based selective access is the key feature:         a doctor granted HIV_TREATMENT access literally cannot read         MENTAL_HEALTH record CIDs from this contract.","kind":"dev","methods":{"addRecord(address,string,uint8,string)":{"details":"Anyone can call this (in the MVP the frontend enforces         that the caller is a clinic). Emits AccessUsed when a         doctor reads records.","params":{"_category":"The category to filter by.","_patient":"The patient whose records to query."},"returns":{"filtered":"Array of MedicalRecord in the requested category."}},"grantAccess(address,uint8,uint256)":{"params":{"_category":"Which record category to share.","_doctor":"The doctor's wallet address.","_durationHours":"How many hours the access lasts."}},"hasActiveAccess(address,address,uint8)":{"returns":{"_0":"True if an active, non-revoked, non-expired grant exists."}},"registerPatient(string)":{"params":{"_name":"A human-readable display name (for the demo)."}},"revokeAccess(address,uint8)":{"params":{"_category":"The category to revoke.","_doctor":"The doctor whose access to revoke."}}},"stateVariables":{"_accessGrants":{"details":"patient wallet \u2192 array of AccessGrant"},"_auditLog":{"details":"Audit log entries per patient."},"_grantIndex":{"details":"Lookup: patient \u2192 doctor \u2192 category \u2192 index in _accessGrants (+ 1).      Zero means \"no grant exists\".  We store index+1 so that 0 is the      sentinel for \"not found\"."},"_patientRecords":{"details":"patient wallet \u2192 array of MedicalRecord"},"patients":{"details":"patient wallet \u2192 Patient metadata"}},"title":"NetsanetCore \u2014 Patient-Owned Medical Records for Ethiopia","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"addRecord(address,string,uint8,string)":{"notice":"A clinic/doctor adds a medical record for a patient."},"getGrantDetails(address,address,uint8)":{"notice":"Convenience: returns grant details for a specific         patient + doctor + category combination."},"getMyAccessGrants()":{"notice":"Returns all access grants for the calling patient."},"getMyAuditLog()":{"notice":"Returns the full audit log for the calling patient."},"getMyRecords()":{"notice":"Returns ALL records for the calling patient (owner only)."},"getRecordCount(address)":{"notice":"Returns the total number of records a patient has."},"getRecordsByCategory(address,uint8)":{"notice":"Returns only the records in `_category` for the given patient."},"grantAccess(address,uint8,uint256)":{"notice":"Patient grants a doctor time-limited access to ONE category."},"hasActiveAccess(address,address,uint8)":{"notice":"Check whether `_doctor` currently has active access to         `_category` for `_patient`."},"registerPatient(string)":{"notice":"Register the caller as a patient."},"revokeAccess(address,uint8)":{"notice":"Patient revokes a doctor's access to a specific category         before it naturally expires."}},"notice":"This contract enables patients to own, store metadata for, and         selectively share their medical records on-chain.  Actual medical         data lives encrypted on IPFS; only CID pointers and access-control         state are stored here.","version":1}},"settings":{"compilationTarget":{"src/NetsanetCore.sol":"NetsanetCore"},"evmVersion":"shanghai","libraries":{},"metadata":{"bytecodeHash":"ipfs"},"optimizer":{"enabled":false,"runs":200},"remappings":[":forge-std/=lib/forge-std/src/"]},"sources":{"src/NetsanetCore.sol":{"keccak256":"0x92bd35216597678829d7e806d6cd2fbe9e5d4c9d7cfaa9479eeff3e6930267f6","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://2d45b0835c751f3e36a0c414c5da15e52e3d3c3a52b3e721e68ac3cb2bb0fcda","dweb:/ipfs/QmTtfss6nweU9FHyt9TnmBLx2aExMdH3ScKXoYs5PidZbU"]}},"version":1}

addRecord(address,string,uint8,string).details: Anyone can call this (in the MVP the frontend enforces         that the caller is a clinic).  The patient can always         verify who added a record via `addedByClinic`.

params._category: The record category (determines who can read it).
params._ipfsCID: IPFS content-identifier of the encrypted record.

returns.exists: Whether a grant was ever created.
returns.grant: The AccessGrant struct (zeroed if none exists).

getMyAccessGrants().details: Only the patient can view their own grants.

getMyAuditLog().details: Only the patient can view their own audit log.

getMyRecords().details: Only the patient themselves can call this.

getRecordsByCategory(address,uint8).details: Caller must either be the patient themselves OR hold an         active grant for that category.  Emits AccessUsed when a         doctor reads records.

params._patient: The patient whose records to query.

returns.filtered: Array of MedicalRecord in the requested category.

params._durationHours: How many hours the access lasts.

returns._0: True if an active, non-revoked, non-expired grant exists.

params._name: A human-readable display name (for the demo).

params._doctor: The doctor whose access to revoke.

addRecord(address,string,uint8,string).notice: A clinic/doctor adds a medical record for a patient.

getGrantDetails(address,address,uint8).notice: Convenience: returns grant details for a specific         patient + doctor + category combination.

getMyAccessGrants().notice: Returns all access grants for the calling patient.

getMyAuditLog().notice: Returns the full audit log for the calling patient.

getMyRecords().notice: Returns ALL records for the calling patient (owner only).

getRecordCount(address).notice: Returns the total number of records a patient has.

getRecordsByCategory(address,uint8).notice: Returns only the records in `_category` for the given patient.

grantAccess(address,uint8,uint256).notice: Patient grants a doctor time-limited access to ONE category.

hasActiveAccess(address,address,uint8).notice: Check whether `_doctor` currently has active access to         `_category` for `_patient`.

registerPatient(string).notice: Register the caller as a patient.

revokeAccess(address,uint8).notice: Patient revokes a doctor's access to a specific category         before it naturally expires.
